title: The Unbound Ledger
subtitle: Chapter 02 · The Wider Fractures
introduction: A merchant's counting house by the docks has ledgers, inkwells, and shipping crates. Focus on the open page.
description: An open inkwell sits on a desk. A merchant's steel nib pen lifts, but the ink print registers a finalized cargo trade transaction inside the ledger book exactly two seconds before the pen touches the paper.
observation: The ledger page fills with black ink, finalizing the trade transaction before the pen makes contact.
reconstruction: Carry the steel nib pen, the open inkwell, and the wax cargo seal.
attunements: The steel nib pen tip is dry and pristine, carrying no residual black ink.
The open inkwell is full, showing no decrease in liquid volume.
The wax cargo seal on the document has solidified, yet carries fresh friction heat.
revelation:
What was wrong: the ledger registered the trade agreement before the pen could touch the page.

Why it mattered: the trade had already been finalized in the merchant's mind, creating a temporal imprint that recorded the agreement before it could be signed.

anomaly_definition.misstep_text: Not there. Focus on the freshly inked page of the ledger book.
anomaly_definition.success_text: Causal transaction detected. The ink prints itself before pen-point contact.

capture_window.guidance_text: HOLD WHEN THE INK PRE-REGISTERS ON THE PAPER.

evidence_nodes.relevance: THE PEN TIP IS DRY AND PRISTINE. The steel nib has not yet absorbed any black ink pigment.
THE INKWELL IS FULL. The ink volume is unchanged, showing no trace of ink draw or evaporation.
THE WAX SEAL CARRIES RESIDUAL FRICTION HEAT. The red wax has hardened, but radiates warm energy.
